Our company culture is built around our company focus/mission: To provide incredible service while making insurance “personal.” What makes IMA unique is our focus on always doing the right thing, being client focused and having a staff/team that is solutions driven.
Eu sint quae recusan
Company Culture

Our Arroyo Redlands office has been perpetually owned/operated as a family business for almost 70 years, currently in its second generation. While the staff and clients receive the service that you would expect from a small family run organization, Arroyo also has wide reach in both the business community and insurance industry through its participation in the Arroyo Insurance Services cluster. The resulting culture is one of empowerment, where individual employees voices are heard by decision makers, and at the same time our organization can compete with the largest agencies in the region on both pricing & service.

Mission Statement
Our Mission is to help our clients protect their company and employees at the lowest net cost of risk and insurance.

Vision
Our efforts help protect human life, property, and corporate assets. Our innovative risk management solutions and services help clients grow their business and increase their profitability. We make business possible by creating a base of strength and stability that clients count on to conduct their business. Our vision is to make your vision a reality.

Corporate Culture
Our culture is based on professionals working together to enable our clients to succeed. We encourage everyone to fulfill their career goals by connecting with the corporate mission. Like all great teams, we are comprised of individuals who work together to accomplish greater goals than they could by working on their own. We value the contributions of everyone and challenge each other to grow and succeed.

Orion empowers each associate to become their best and grow their career through professional development and education. We also reward everyone for their efforts and respect each person’s individuality.

We believe that a meaningful career is one that starts with adding value to others so that our efforts matter in a greater sense. A successful career also includes personal growth in terms of career advancement, daily enjoyment and increasing income.

Orion provides its associates with a very comprehensive employee benefits package including; major medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, life insurance, and a 401k plan with employer match. We also provide generous PTO and career flexibility for professional and personal life balance.

Small casual atmosphere.   We focus on providing exceptional service and value to our customers.  I believe and live the golden rule.. Treat others as you want to be treated.   
 We understand that we should NOT write every piece of business.  Not every potential client is a fit for our agency or us for them.    If we can not provide the best product at a great price we will gladly help the client find a product with our competition.    There is enough business out in the world.  We do not need to pound a square peg into a round hole.

A Certified Managing General Agency serving independent agencies throughout the Southeast for over fifty years.

The Dillhoff Agency was founded in 1905; the Geile Hils & Kruse Agency began in 1937 and the Kneflin Agency was established in 1953. Due to our ongoing desire to better serve our clients, we combined as one agency in November 1992. The Company has grown to a full-service Insurance Agency. Currently, the Staff includes Account Executives, Account Managers and Administrative Assistants. When choosing an insurance agency, you want a company you can trust. We are an independent insurance agency. We do not work for an insurance company; we work for you! We work on your side when you have a loss and follow through to see that you get fair, prompt payment. As an independent, full-service insurance agency, our duty is to our clients best interests. As your insurance agents, it is our responsibility to provide YOU the best service and the best coverage, at the best possible price. We are a little old fashioned, if you want something done quickly, we will do everything we can to assist. Customer service is of paramount importance to us. Our customers recommend us to their friends. Our expertise is based on well-trained experienced staff that can tailor a comprehensive insurance program for your specific needs. You will have the added benefit of working with agents who understand your needs. Finding the right policy is only the beginning.

top_values.overall_summary: The candidate presents only a high school diploma with no documented work experience, skills, licenses, or industry knowledge relevant to the E&O Account Manager position. The resume lacks all critical elements required for this role, including insurance industry experience, Professional Lines and E&O expertise, Property & Casualty licensing, client service background, and technical proficiency with insurance management systems. While no items were marked as mandatory in the JD, the complete absence of any qualifying experience, skills, or credentials makes this candidate unsuitable for a specialized commercial lines account management role that requires intermediate-level insurance expertise.

The candidate cannot be recommended for this position due to the absence of a mandatory Property & Casualty insurance license. While they possess strong sales experience from the telecommunications sector and demonstrate excellent relationship-building and customer service skills, the lack of required insurance licensing is a critical gap. The candidate would need to obtain the P&C license before being considered for this insurance sales role.

The candidate cannot be considered for this Commercial Lines Account Manager role due to the absence of a required Property & Casualty insurance license, which is a mandatory qualification. While the candidate demonstrates strong administrative, customer service, and organizational skills from diverse industry experience, they lack the foundational insurance licensing required to perform the core responsibilities of this position. Without this credential, no further evaluation of skills or experience is warranted.

The candidate possesses extensive insurance industry experience spanning 17 years, with deep expertise in P&C underwriting, sales leadership, and team management. However, they do not meet the mandatory licensing requirement for this Commercial Lines Account Executive role. The absence of a documented Property & Casualty license is a critical disqualifier, preventing further evaluation of their otherwise strong qualifications in insurance operations, client management, and commercial insurance knowledge.

The candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this position. They lack the mandatory Property & Casualty license and have not provided evidence of meeting the required High School Diploma (or equivalent) education level. Despite having 18 years of insurance industry experience with strong expertise in commercial lines account management, policy renewals, and AMS360 proficiency, the absence of these fundamental mandatory credentials disqualifies them from consideration for this role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for an account management role, with proven insurance agency experience, active P&C licensing, and excellent client service capabilities. They excel in core job functions (policy administration, client relationships, retention) and possess key interpersonal traits (customer orientation, communication, detail orientation). However, there is a significant gap in specialized Professional Lines expertise—the candidate's background is exclusively in Property & Casualty, while this role requires experience with E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL, and M&A coverages. Additionally, the candidate lacks documented proficiency with standard office software (MS Office suite, Adobe) and specific tools like AMS360 and DocuSign. The candidate would be a strong fit for a P&C account management role but would require substantial training and development to handle the specialized Professional Lines focus of this position.

This candidate is a strong fit for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role with 23 years of property and casualty insurance experience and an active P&C license. They demonstrate excellent alignment with core job functions including client renewals, certificate processing, quoting, and policy reviews, and are proficient in AMS360 (Applied Systems). Key strengths include extensive commercial lines account management experience (800+ accounts) and specialized construction sector expertise. Notable gaps include lack of preferred industry certifications (ACSR, CIC, CLCS), incomplete Bachelor's degree, missing explicit evidence of specific coverage expertise (property, casualty, WC, professional liability), and limited documentation of soft skills beyond customer orientation and teamwork. Despite these gaps, the candidate's extensive practical experience and proven track record make them a recommended hire for this position.

The candidate is a highly experienced P&C insurance professional with 23+ years in the industry and meets all mandatory requirements. They demonstrate strong transferable skills in policy administration, customer service, quoting, rating, and agency management systems. However, there is a significant gap: the role requires personal lines expertise (auto, homeowners, renters, umbrella), while the candidate's experience is entirely in commercial lines with specialization in construction. The candidate would need training and transition support to move from commercial to personal lines, though their foundational insurance knowledge, customer orientation, and technical skills provide a solid foundation for success with proper onboarding.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational insurance experience with 12 years in commercial lines, excellent client relationship management skills, and proven policy administration capabilities. They possess the required P&C license and have relevant brokerage and account management experience. However, there is a critical gap in Professional Lines expertise—specifically E&O, Cyber, D&O, and EPLI—which is central to this role. The candidate's background is heavily weighted toward commercial casualty lines (Auto, GL, Workers Comp) rather than the Professional Lines focus this position requires. While their transferable skills in client service, policy management, and relationship building are strong, they would need significant training and development in the Professional Lines arena to be fully successful in this E&O Account Manager role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational insurance experience with 5+ years in account management, policy administration, and client service within the insurance brokerage sector. They possess the required P&C license and proficiency in Microsoft Office tools. However, there is a significant gap in the specialized areas of expertise required for this E&O Account Manager role—the candidate's background is in property & casualty and student accident insurance rather than professional lines (E&O, D&O, Cyber, EPLI). Additionally, they lack experience with preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign) and have limited documentation of several desired soft skills. While the candidate has transferable account management and client service skills, they would require substantial training in professional lines insurance to fully succeed in this role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational skills in client relationship management, insurance policy servicing, and possesses the required Property & Casualty license. They excel in behavioral traits such as customer orientation, teamwork, and problem-solving. However, they lack specialized Professional Lines insurance expertise (E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL) which is central to this role, and have no documented experience with key management systems (AMS360, DocuSign) or specific computer applications. The candidate's insurance background is primarily in Property & Casualty rather than Professional Lines, representing a significant knowledge gap for an E&O Account Manager position. While their transferable skills in account management and client service are valuable, they would require substantial training in Professional Lines products and systems to succeed in this role.

This candidate is a strong fit for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role with 5 years of relevant P&C insurance experience, including 2 years in commercial lines account management. They hold the mandatory P&C license and exceed the education requirement with a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics. Key strengths include demonstrated expertise in client renewals, portfolio management, multi-carrier negotiation, and proficiency with AMS 360. Notable gaps include lack of explicit certificate processing experience, preferred insurance designations (ACSR/CIC/CLCS), and some specialized coverage areas (workers' comp, professional liability). The candidate's analytical background and proven track record managing a $3M+ portfolio with 98% retention make them well-suited for this role despite some preferred skill gaps.

This candidate is a strong fit for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role with 18 years of directly relevant experience in commercial property and casualty insurance. They excel in core job functions including renewals, certificates, documentation, client service, and quoting, and hold the mandatory P&C license. Key strengths include expert-level proficiency in Applied Systems (TAM), EPIC, and Sagitta management systems, plus demonstrated customer orientation, detail orientation, and communication skills. Notable gaps include lack of professional certifications (ACSR, CIC, CLCS), missing documentation of some soft skills (relationship building, teamwork, initiative), no explicit experience with Professional Liability/E&O or Workers' Compensation, and absence of listed computer skills for standard office software. Despite these gaps in preferred qualifications, the candidate's extensive hands-on experience and proven track record in commercial lines account management make them a recommended hire.

The candidate demonstrates solid foundational experience in insurance brokerage with 4 years as an Insurance Agent and Customer Service Representative, showing strong alignment with the client service aspects of the role. However, they lack the specialized Professional Lines expertise that is central to this E&O Account Manager position, including no documented experience with Errors & Omissions, Cyber Liability, D&O, or other management liability coverages. Additionally, the absence of a P&C license, AMS360 experience, and limited documented soft skills present notable gaps. While the candidate has relevant industry background and customer service strengths, they would require significant training and development in the specialized coverage areas to succeed in this role.

The candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this role. They lack the mandatory Property & Casualty license and have not provided evidence of the required High School Diploma or equivalent. Without these foundational credentials, the candidate cannot be considered for the Commercial Lines Account Executive position, despite having relevant insurance industry experience.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational skills in customer service and general insurance operations with 4 years of relevant experience. However, there are significant gaps in the specialized expertise required for this E&O Account Manager role, particularly in Professional Lines, Errors & Omissions, and related specialty coverages. The candidate lacks documentation of technical systems (AMS360, DocuSign) and does not hold a documented P&C license. While their client service orientation aligns well with the role's relationship management requirements, the absence of specialized commercial lines and E&O experience represents a substantial training investment that would be needed for success in this position.

The candidate meets all mandatory requirements with a valid Property & Casualty license and education exceeding the minimum. They demonstrate strong alignment with core job functions through 4 years of relevant insurance experience and hold the preferred CIC certification. However, significant gaps exist in Commercial Lines specialization, technical expertise areas (Workers' Compensation, Professional Liability, Carrier Rating Systems), management systems (Applied Systems), and several desired soft skills. The candidate shows foundational insurance competency but lacks the specialized Commercial Lines depth and technical proficiencies that would make them an ideal fit for this Account Executive role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational skills in insurance with an active P&C license and 4 years of client-facing experience in insurance sales and consultation. They excel in core competencies like client service, relationship management, and possess key traits such as communication, detail orientation, and customer focus. However, significant gaps exist in specialized areas critical to this E&O Account Manager role: they lack experience with Professional Lines products (E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL), have no documented experience with required management systems (AMS360, DocuSign), and their background is in P&C rather than the Commercial Lines/Professional Lines focus of this position. While they have transferable skills, the specialized nature of E&O account management and the lack of relevant product expertise make this a borderline fit requiring substantial training and development.

This candidate is highly qualified for the Account Executive, Commercial Lines Services role with strong commercial insurance experience managing a $3.5M+ book and 94% client retention. They possess the required P&C license, Bachelor's degree, AMS360 proficiency, and demonstrate excellent client management and strategic capabilities. While they lack some specialized areas like property underwriting proficiency, Commercial Auto/Umbrella expertise, and the CPRM certification, their solid foundation in commercial lines insurance, proven track record in account management, and strong technical skills make them an excellent fit for this position.

The candidate meets the mandatory P&C license requirement and demonstrates strong transferable skills in account management, customer service, and sales from their food distribution background. However, they lack all insurance-specific technical expertise, including commercial lines knowledge, policy operations experience, and familiarity with insurance management systems. While their relationship-building abilities and Microsoft Office proficiency are assets, the absence of any insurance industry experience represents a significant gap. This is a career-transition candidate who would require substantial training and onboarding to succeed in this commercial lines account manager role.

The candidate is a licensed P&C professional with 6 years of insurance experience who meets all mandatory requirements and demonstrates strong foundational skills in personal lines sales, client service, and relationship building. However, significant gaps exist in specialized coverage areas (umbrella, recreational vehicles, high-value homes), required agency management systems (AMS360, AgencyZoom), and documented computer skills. The candidate falls short of the preferred 5 years of inside sales experience with only 1 year documented. While the core insurance sales competencies are present, the lack of technical system proficiency and specialized product knowledge may require substantial training and onboarding support.

The candidate brings 18 years of insurance industry experience with strong management, coaching, customer service, and operational skills. They demonstrate excellent leadership capabilities and relationship management, currently working in an insurance brokerage setting. However, significant gaps exist in Personal Lines-specific product knowledge (Homeowners, Renters, Personal Auto), required insurance licenses (PL License, P&C), technical systems (EPIC, MS Office suite), and documented sales/production achievement experience. While the candidate has transferable management and customer service skills, they would require substantial training and licensing to fully meet the Personal Lines Manager role requirements.

The candidate meets the mandatory P&C license requirement and demonstrates solid foundational experience in insurance operations, claims administration, and policy servicing. They show strong alignment with job functions (88%) and subindustry requirements (100%), with a Bachelor's degree exceeding education preferences. However, significant gaps exist in specialized product knowledge (only 25% match in areas of expertise), preferred management systems (0% match), and documented soft skills. The candidate's general insurance background provides a good foundation, but they would require training on specific product lines and agency systems to fully succeed in this administrative assistant role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational insurance experience with 3 years in Property & Casualty insurance, excellent customer service skills, and the required P&C license. However, there are significant gaps in specialized Professional Lines expertise (E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL) which is central to this Commercial Lines Services role. The candidate also lacks documented experience with preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign) and several desired soft skills. While the candidate shows promise in core insurance operations and client service, the transition from Personal Lines P&C to Commercial Professional Lines would require substantial training and development.

The candidate brings strong technical insurance expertise with 13 years in the industry, including deep knowledge of professional lines, E&O, cyber, and D&O coverage. They hold an active P&C license and demonstrate excellent attention to detail and analytical skills. However, their experience is primarily in carrier-side underwriting support and claims rather than retail broker account management. The role requires client-facing relationship management, service delivery, and retention skills that are not evidenced in the resume. The candidate would need to transition from technical operations to client-facing account management, which represents a significant role shift. While their insurance knowledge is solid, the lack of agency/broker experience and account management background makes this a borderline fit.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for this Administrative Assistant role, meeting the mandatory P&C license requirement and showing excellent alignment with required traits and job functions. They have extensive insurance industry experience in account management, policy administration, and client servicing. However, significant gaps exist in specialized product knowledge (Builder's Risk, specific personal lines products, Workers' Comp) and required technology systems (Agency Matrix, DocuSign, Zoom, Adobe). The candidate's education level also falls below the preferred standard. While their transferable skills and insurance background are solid, the lack of specific technical expertise and systems knowledge places them in the borderline category for this particular role.

The candidate brings 12+ years of solid commercial insurance experience with strong capabilities in account management, policy servicing, renewals, and client relationship management—all core functions for this role. However, there is a significant gap in Professional Lines expertise, particularly in Errors & Omissions, which is the primary focus of this position. The candidate demonstrates 3 of 4 required computer skills and several key soft skills (customer orientation, detail orientation, problem solving) but lacks experience with the preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign) and does not hold a P&C license. While their foundational insurance operations skills are strong, the absence of specialized Professional Lines experience makes this a borderline fit that would require substantial training in E&O, D&O, Cyber, and other professional liability coverages.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational skills in insurance operations, data management, and core professional traits like detail orientation and communication. With 7 years at Marsh in insurance operations, they have relevant industry experience and excellent technical proficiency with databases and Excel. However, significant gaps exist in specialized Professional Lines knowledge (E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL), the preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign), and the Property & Casualty license. The candidate would require substantial training and development in Professional Lines insurance products to succeed in this E&O Account Manager role, making them a borderline fit who could potentially grow into the position with proper support and mentorship.

The candidate is a solid fit for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role with strong foundational qualifications. They possess the mandatory P&C license and demonstrate 5 years of expert-level commercial insurance experience, particularly in commercial auto and truck insurance. Key strengths include account management capabilities, underwriting expertise, relevant management systems knowledge (AMS/CRM), and strong interpersonal traits (customer service, communication, teamwork, decision-making). However, the candidate's experience is heavily specialized in commercial auto/truck insurance and lacks documented expertise in several preferred commercial lines areas such as property, casualty/general liability, professional liability, and workers' compensation. The absence of industry certifications (ACSR, CIC, CLCS) and a bachelor's degree are notable gaps, though not disqualifying. With some training in broader commercial lines products and certificate processing, this candidate could successfully transition into this role.

The candidate is a strong fit for this E&O Account Manager role with 5+ years of recent commercial lines account management experience, P&C licensing, and demonstrated expertise in client relationship management and service delivery. Key strengths include professional liability insurance background, Applied EPIC proficiency (transferable to AMS360), and documented excellence in customer orientation and detail orientation. Notable gaps include lack of specific E&O, cyber, D&O, and EPLI expertise, though the candidate's professional lines foundation provides a solid base for learning these specialty areas. The candidate's proven track record in account management, retention, and operational excellence makes them well-suited for this role despite some missing preferred specialty line experience.

This candidate has extensive and impressive experience in the insurance industry, particularly in property and casualty underwriting, brokerage, and commercial insurance. However, their background is fundamentally misaligned with the requirements of this role, which seeks a technical QA professional with expertise in production systems stability, software testing, and DevOps collaboration. The candidate demonstrates strong relationship building, customer orientation, and goal-oriented traits, and has relevant insurance industry experience, but lacks any technical QA, software testing, or production systems experience. This represents a complete domain mismatch between insurance underwriting and technical quality assurance.

This candidate demonstrates strong qualifications for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role with 15 years of insurance industry experience and the required P&C license. Key strengths include extensive quoting and rating experience, proficiency in Applied Systems, commercial lines expertise, and strong customer service orientation. The candidate shows solid performance in core job functions like managing renewals, documentation, and client service. Notable gaps include missing specific experience in certificates/EOPs, casualty/general liability, property, and workers' compensation, as well as lacking preferred certifications (ACSR, CIC, CLCS) and a bachelor's degree. Overall, the candidate meets mandatory requirements and demonstrates sufficient capability for the role, though some preferred qualifications are absent.

The candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this position. They lack the mandatory Property & Casualty license and have not provided evidence of the required High School Diploma or equivalent education. Without these fundamental qualifications, the candidate cannot be considered for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role, despite having relevant insurance experience in their background.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for this Personal Lines Inside Sales Associate role, with 13 years of insurance industry experience, active P&C licensing, and proven sales success. Key strengths include relationship building, goal orientation, customer service excellence, and property damage expertise. However, notable gaps exist in personal lines product knowledge (auto, umbrella, specialty vehicles), agency management systems (AMS360, AgencyZoom), and documented computer skills. The candidate's claims adjusting background provides valuable insurance knowledge but may require training on personal lines sales processes and agency-specific systems. With proper onboarding and product training, this candidate could transition successfully into the inside sales role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications with 7 years of insurance industry experience, excellent client service capabilities, and relevant operational management skills. Key strengths include customer orientation, detail orientation, problem-solving abilities, and property insurance licensing. However, the candidate lacks specialized professional lines expertise—particularly in Errors & Omissions, Cyber Liability, D&O, and EPL—which are central to this E&O Account Manager role. Additionally, the resume does not document experience with preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign) or explicitly list computer skills, though these may be present but undocumented. The candidate would benefit from professional lines training and system familiarization but has a solid insurance foundation and transferable account management skills that could support success in this role with appropriate onboarding.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational capabilities for this E&O Account Manager role, with excellent soft skills, client relationship management experience, and current insurance sector background. Their 3 years as an Associate Risk Advisor shows relevant account servicing, client management, and analytical skills that align well with the core job functions. However, the candidate lacks specialized Professional Lines insurance expertise (E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPLI) and does not hold a P&C license, which are preferred qualifications. They also have no documented experience with industry-specific systems like AMS360. The candidate would likely require training in Professional Lines products but brings transferable skills and the right behavioral traits to succeed in the role.

This candidate is a strong fit for the Commercial Lines Account Executive role with 20 years of insurance industry experience and 8 years specifically as a Commercial Account Executive. They meet all mandatory requirements including P&C licensing and education, and hold valuable certifications including CIC and CISR. Key strengths include extensive renewal management, client relationship skills, Applied EPIC proficiency, and deep commercial lines experience. Notable gaps include lack of explicit expertise in several specialized coverage areas (casualty/GL, workers' comp, professional liability), missing some preferred certifications (ACSR, CLCS), and undocumented proficiency in standard office software tools. The candidate's proven track record in account management and commercial insurance operations makes them well-suited for this role despite some documentation gaps in preferred skills.

The candidate demonstrates solid commercial insurance account management fundamentals with 5+ years of experience, appropriate licensing, and strong alignment with core job functions including renewals, client service, and relationship management. However, there is a critical gap in specialized professional lines expertise—specifically E&O, Professional Lines, D&O, and EPLI—which are central to this E&O Account Manager role. The candidate's experience is primarily in general commercial lines and personal lines rather than the professional liability focus this position requires. While the candidate shows strong foundational skills and could potentially transition into this role with training, the lack of specialized professional lines background represents a significant development need.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for an account management role, including active P&C licensing, current AMS360 proficiency, and proven client relationship management skills. However, there is a significant mismatch in specialized expertise: the role requires professional lines experience (E&O, D&O, Cyber, EPLI), while the candidate's insurance background is concentrated in commercial auto and transportation lines. The candidate's exceptional problem-solving abilities, leadership experience, and strong behavioral traits partially offset this technical gap. This candidate could succeed in the role with targeted training in professional lines products, but would require a learning curve to develop the specialized E&O and management liability expertise the position demands.

The candidate is disqualified from consideration due to not holding the mandatory Property & Casualty insurance license required for this Personal Lines Inside Sales Associate role. While the candidate has relevant sales experience in medical and life insurance and meets the educational requirement, the absence of the required P&C license is a critical gap that prevents further evaluation. The candidate would need to obtain this license before being considered for this position.

However, significant gaps exist in commercial lines-specific expertise, with no documented experience in key areas like property, casualty, workers' compensation, or certificate processing. The resume lacks evidence of technical skills in preferred systems (Applied Systems) and does not detail commercial lines specialization, which is central to this role. While the candidate's transferable skills are strong, the absence of explicit commercial lines experience creates uncertainty about role-specific readiness.

The candidate would need to obtain this license before being considered for this role.

They possess the required P&C license, proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, and proven client service capabilities. However, there is a significant gap in Professional Lines expertise—the candidate's background is concentrated in commercial and personal lines for contractors/homebuilders rather than the specialized E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL, and M&A coverages this role emphasizes. While the candidate has transferable account management skills and could potentially learn these product lines, the lack of Professional Lines experience represents a notable training requirement. The candidate would be a solid fit for general commercial account management but may need substantial onboarding for the specialized Professional Lines focus of this E&O-centered position.

The candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this Commercial Lines Account Manager position. They lack the mandatory Property & Casualty insurance license required for the role. Their background is entirely in healthcare (medical assistant, phlebotomist, emergency medicine) with no insurance industry experience. While they demonstrate strong customer service skills and organizational abilities from their healthcare roles, their qualifications are fundamentally misaligned with the insurance industry requirements of this position.

However, the resume shows critical gaps in specialized areas of expertise required for this E&O Account Manager role, particularly lacking Professional Lines and Errors & Omissions experience at the intermediate level. Additionally, the candidate does not document essential technical skills (AMS360, MS Office suite), a Property & Casualty license, or any of the desired candidate traits. Key strengths include handling high call volumes, working with 40+ carriers, and using multiple insurance management systems including EPIC. Notable gaps include lack of documented experience with some preferred computer skills (MS Office suite) and management systems (CSR24, Zywave, Applied Rater), as well as limited evidence of specific expertise in personal auto and liability/umbrella products. Overall, the candidate's proven track record in personal lines sales and service, combined with strong customer orientation and problem-solving skills, makes them a recommended candidate for this position.

The candidate demonstrates problem-solving and decision-making abilities but lacks the core technical QA skills, production stability experience, and industry background (insurance/financial services) that this position demands. Their expertise in business analytics and marketplace advertising does not translate to the production validation and system monitoring responsibilities of this role.

The candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this position. While they possess extensive commercial insurance experience, professional designations (ASLI, AU), and relevant expertise in property and casualty underwriting, they lack the mandatory Property & Casualty license required by the job description. Without this fundamental credential, the candidate cannot be considered for the role regardless of their other qualifications.

This candidate has a strong background in healthcare with over 7 years of experience in emergency medicine, patient care, and medical support services. However, they completely lack the mandatory Property & Casualty insurance license required for this Commercial Lines Account Executive role. Additionally, the candidate has no experience in insurance operations, commercial lines, policy management, or any of the insurance-specific functions outlined in the job description. Their skill set is entirely focused on healthcare and medical services, making them fundamentally misaligned with this insurance industry position.

The candidate brings 25 years of strong commercial insurance account management experience with expert-level proficiency in renewals, client relationship management, and service delivery. They hold the required P&C license and demonstrate key traits including customer orientation, detail orientation, and initiative. However, a significant gap exists in Professional Lines expertise (E&O, D&O, Cyber, EPLI) which is central to this E&O Account Manager role. The candidate also lacks documented experience with preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign) and computer skills, though these may be trainable. With their strong foundational insurance skills and client service background, the candidate could potentially transition into this role with appropriate training in Professional Lines coverages.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for this E&O Account Manager role with 18 years of insurance experience, including specific E&O expertise, P&C licensing, and extensive commercial and personal lines account management. Key strengths include proven client service capabilities, underwriting experience, and 11 years as an independent agent. However, the candidate shows gaps in specialized professional lines areas (Cyber, D&O, EPL), lacks documented experience with preferred management systems (AMS360, DocuSign), and has limited documentation of several desired soft skills. The candidate's solid insurance fundamentals and E&O background make them a viable fit, though additional training in specialized professional lines and agency management systems would be beneficial.

The candidate is a strong fit for the account management and client service aspects of this role, with 5+ years of commercial insurance experience, relevant licensing, and proficiency in AMS360. They demonstrate excellent foundational skills in renewals, policy servicing, client relationships, and general commercial account management. However, the role specifically focuses on E&O and professional lines, where the candidate lacks documented expertise. The candidate's experience is primarily in general commercial lines and personal lines rather than the specialized professional liability coverages (E&O, D&O, EPLI) that are central to this position. With training and support in professional lines, this candidate could transition successfully given their strong service foundation and insurance acumen.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational insurance experience through agency ownership, policy administration, and client relationship management, along with the required P&C license. They excel in job functions, subindustry alignment, and possess key traits like customer orientation and problem-solving. However, they lack the specialized Professional Lines expertise (E&O, Cyber, D&O, EPL) that is central to this role, and do not explicitly demonstrate several required computer skills and soft skills. While their transferable insurance operations experience is valuable, the absence of Commercial Lines and Professional Lines specialization represents a significant gap for this E&O-focused Account Manager position.

The candidate demonstrates solid foundational insurance experience with 3 years as an independent agency owner, active P&C licensing, and strong client service capabilities. However, there is a critical gap in Professional Lines expertise—specifically E&O, Cyber, D&O, and EPL coverage areas that are central to this E&O Account Manager role. While the candidate shows transferable skills in CRM systems, policy administration, and client relationship management, the lack of specialized Professional Lines experience and limited documentation of several desired traits and computer skills places them in the borderline category. This candidate could potentially succeed with significant training and mentorship in Professional Lines products.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for an account management role, including active P&C licensing, 3 years of current commercial insurance experience, and proven proficiency with AMS360. They excel in client relationship management, policy administration, and renewals processing. However, there is a significant gap in the specialized areas of expertise required for this E&O-focused position—the candidate's background is in commercial auto and transportation insurance rather than professional lines (E&O, D&O, Cyber, EPLI). While their transferable skills in account management and client service are strong, they would require training and development in professional liability products to fully succeed in this role.

The candidate demonstrates strong foundational qualifications for an account management role, including active P&C licensing, AMS360 proficiency, and proven client service capabilities in commercial insurance. They possess excellent behavioral traits including problem-solving, judgment, and customer orientation. However, a critical gap exists in the areas of expertise: the candidate's insurance experience is entirely focused on commercial auto and transportation lines, with no background in professional lines, E&O, cyber liability, D&O, or other management liability coverages that are central to this E&O Account Manager position. While their transferable skills in policy administration, renewals, and client relationship management are solid, the lack of professional lines experience represents a significant learning curve that would need to be addressed through training and mentorship.
